title: Bazaar Ware Manifest
description: Schema for manifest.json — the only WordPress-specific file in a Bazaar ware. Place manifest.json at the root of your .wp archive.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference. Set to the $id of this schema for IDE validation.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the Bazaar gallery and browser tab.

slug.description: Permanent unique identifier. Used as the directory name, REST path segment, and menu slug. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Cannot be changed after installation.

version.description: Semver-style version string. Displayed in the gallery and compared against registry.updateUrl to detect updates.

author.description: Creator name shown in the gallery card.

description.description: Short description shown in the gallery card. One or two sentences.
examples: Generate and manage invoices directly from wp-admin.

icon.description: Path to the sidebar icon relative to the archive root. SVG recommended (embedded as data URI, scales cleanly). Falls back to dashicons-admin-plugins if missing.

entry.description: Path to the HTML entry file relative to the archive root. Bazaar loads this in the sandboxed iframe.

menu.description: Controls how the ware's WordPress admin page is registered. All sub-fields are optional.

title.description: Label shown in the WordPress sidebar. Defaults to the top-level name field.

position.description: WordPress menu position integer. Use non-round numbers (e.g. 26 instead of 25) to avoid collisions.

capability.description: WordPress capability required to see and access the ware. Enforced on both menu registration and the REST file-serving endpoint.

parent.description: When set, the ware becomes a submenu item under an existing top-level menu page slug (e.g. 'tools.php', 'options-general.php'). null = top-level item.

group.description: Arbitrary label used by the Bazaar shell to visually cluster related wares in the sidebar. Multiple wares with the same group value are rendered under a shared section header.

permissions.description: WordPress capability tokens this ware declares it needs. Displayed to the admin during installation. Unrecognised tokens are silently dropped.

permissions_network.description: Network access policy. null = no outbound fetch allowed (default). true = allow all outbound. Array = HTTPS URL allowlist. The WordPress site origin is always implicitly allowed.

oneOf.description: Allow all outbound requests. Use only for trusted wares.
Allowlist of HTTPS origins/URLs the ware may fetch from.

shared.description: npm package names to load from the Bazaar shell's import map instead of bundling. Reduces download size — the browser caches shared packages across all wares. IMPORTANT: any package listed here must also be in rollupOptions.external in vite.config.ts.

health_check.description: URL Bazaar polls to determine whether an external dependency is reachable. Result is surfaced as ok/warn/error in the shell UI.

jobs.description: Background jobs Bazaar schedules via WP-Cron on install.

id.description: Unique job identifier within this ware.

label.description: Human-readable description shown in the Bazaar shell.

interval.description: WP-Cron schedule name. Built-in options: hourly, twicedaily, daily, weekly. Custom schedule names are also accepted.

endpoint.description: REST URL Bazaar calls when the job fires. Omit if you register a WP-Cron hook directly in a companion plugin.

url.description: URL Bazaar POSTs { slug, key, site } to for remote validation.

required.description: When true, installation is blocked until a valid key is stored.

registry.description: Links this ware to a remote registry for update checks and gallery listings.

updateUrl.description: URL to a JSON file describing the latest available version. Must return at least a 'version' field.

homepage.description: Canonical page for this ware, shown in the gallery.

signature.description: Base64-encoded RSA signature over the archive. Verified by Bazaar on install when a keypair is configured.
